question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can a user who passes a TokenPermit with PermitKind.ERC2612 for a loan token where the permit is signed for maxBuyerAssets but the bundler's safeTransfer(loanToken, msg.sender, maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets) at the end uses the loan token's transfer() which has a fee-on-transfer, under the precondition that the return transfer charges a fee, trigger the sequence [permit pulls maxBuyerAssets -> takes fill -> referralFeeAssets sent -> bundler calls safeTransfer(loanToken, msg.sender, excess) -> token charges fee on transfer -> user receives excess - fee instead of excess], violating the invariant that the user must receive back exactly the unused portion of maxBuyerAssets, causing scoped impact: user loses the fee-on-transfer amount on the refund, constituting a direct user loss? Proof idea: fuzz fee rate, assert user's received refund equals maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken] Can a user who passes a TokenPermit with PermitKind.ERC2612 for a token that has a permit() function that is only callable by the token owner (msg.sender == owner), under the precondition that the bundler calls permit() on behalf of the user (msg.sender == bundler, not user), trigger the sequence [permit() reverts because msg.sender != owner -> try/catch swallows revert -> safeTransferFrom proceeds without allowance -> reverts], violating the invariant that the bundler must be able to call permit() on behalf of the user, causing scoped impact: user's bundle reverts and their intended Midnight interaction is blocked for tokens with owner-only permit? Proof idea: deploy owner-only permit mock, assert pullToken reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can a user who passes a TokenPermit with PermitKind.ERC2612 for a loan token where the permit is signed for maxBuyerAssets but the bundler's try/catch around take() catches a revert that was caused by the bundler's own logic error (not an asynchrony issue), under the precondition that the take() revert is due to a bug in ConsumableUnitsLib or TakeAmountsLib, trigger the sequence [permit pulls maxBuyerAssets -> take() reverts due to library bug -> try/catch catches revert -> filledUnits remains 0 -> require(filledUnits == targetUnits) reverts -> bundle reverts -> user's permit nonce is consumed], violating the invariant that the bundler must not silently swallow reverts caused by its own logic errors, causing scoped impact: user's permit nonce is consumed and their intended Midnight interaction is blocked without a clear error? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken] Can a user who passes a TokenPermit with PermitKind.ERC2612 for a token that has a permit() function that is paused (reverts when the token is paused), under the precondition that the token is paused at the time of the bundle execution, trigger the sequence [permit() reverts due to pause -> try/catch swallows revert -> safeTransferFrom() also reverts due to pause -> bundle reverts with a misleading error], violating the invariant that the bundler must propagate token pause errors clearly rather than swallowing them, causing scoped impact: user's bundle reverts with a confusing error and they cannot diagnose that the token is paused?
